findings.description: Empty catch blocks silently swallow errors: `catch (_) {}` appears at lines 228, 254, 272. Debugging is impossible when errors are silently ignored.
findings.recommendation: At minimum log the error: `catch (e) { console.error('zapService context:', e); }`. For WebSocket close errors, log at debug level.

findings.description: Empty catch blocks `catch (_) {}` at lines 117, 138, 173 suppress errors silently during store-building operations.
findings.recommendation: Log errors with context. Use `catch (e) { logger.error('storeBuilderService failed:', e); throw e; }` unless the failure is genuinely ignorable.

findings.description: Unhandled promise rejections using `.catch(() => {})` pattern at lines 559, 420, 510. `await orderService.failOrder(orderId, error.message).catch(() => {})` silently ignores failures when marking orders failed.
findings.recommendation: Replace with `.catch(err => console.error('Failed to update order status:', orderId, err))` to at minimum log the secondary failure.

findings.description: Magic numbers scattered throughout: `windowMs: 60 * 60 * 1000` (rate limit window), `Math.round((usdAmount / 60000) * 1e8)` (hardcoded BTC price estimate of $60k), `windowMs: 60 * 1000, max: 30` at line 1022. The BTC price hardcode is especially dangerous.
findings.recommendation: Extract to named constants at top of file: `const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000; const CHECKOUT_RATE_LIMIT_MAX = 10;`. Remove hardcoded BTC price — fetch from live API or config.

findings.description: DB helper functions `dbGet`, `dbAll`, `dbRun` are copy-pasted identically in cryptoCheckout.js (lines 80-94) and courseRoutes.js (lines 35-58) and adminRoutes.js. This duplication is a maintenance hazard.
findings.recommendation: Create `marketplace/backend/services/databaseHelper.js` with promisified wrappers. Import everywhere instead of redefining.

findings.description: Core L402 functionality marked TODO and unimplemented: 'TODO: port from C:\code\arxmint\app\api\l402\route.ts' and 'TODO: implement once ArxMint SDK is available' at lines 38, 41, 61, 64, 83, 86. Methods exist as stubs that throw at runtime.
findings.recommendation: Either implement or remove stubs entirely. If planned, document in docs/PLANNED_FEATURES.md and remove the throwing stubs to prevent silent runtime failures.

findings.description: NFT smart contract calls stubbed with TODOs at lines 237, 296, 436: 'TODO: Call smart contract when deployed'. These are user-facing features that silently do nothing.
findings.recommendation: Return explicit 501 Not Implemented responses or remove the routes until the feature is ready.

findings.description: 1000+ console.log calls across 102+ files with no structured logging. No timestamps, severity levels, or correlation IDs. Critical payment events (Stripe webhooks, order creation) use the same channel as debug prints.
findings.recommendation: Implement Winston or Pino logger with log levels (error/warn/info/debug). Replace console.log in routes/services with logger.info(). Add request correlation IDs for tracing.

findings.description: emailService.js is 1151 lines. checkout.js is 1077 lines. storefront.js is 932 lines. adminRoutes.js is 1120 lines. Functions within these files exceed 100 lines with deeply nested conditionals.
findings.recommendation: Break emailService.js into transactional/, marketing/, and template/ modules. Extract checkout business logic to a dedicated checkoutService. Target <200 lines per file, <50 lines per function.

findings.description: Inconsistent error response formats across routes. adminRoutes returns `{error: '...'}` without `success` field (line 241). checkout.js returns `{success: false, error: '...'}` OR `{success: false, stripeDown: true, fallbackUrl: '...'}`. storeBuilder returns `{error: '...'}`. Clients must handle multiple shapes.
findings.recommendation: Standardize to `{success: boolean, code: string, message: string, data?: object}` with an error code enum. Create a `sendError(res, code, message)` helper.

findings.description: Missing input validation: `format` parameter from user not validated against allowed formats before catalog lookup. Missing schema validation at route entry points throughout.
findings.recommendation: Add Zod or Joi schema validation at route handlers. Example: `const schema = z.object({ format: z.enum(['pdf','epub','print']) })`.

findings.description: Race condition in lazy Stripe initialization: `let _stripe = null; if (!_stripe) { _stripe = require('stripe')(key); }` — concurrent requests can trigger duplicate initialization. Same pattern in aiDiscoveryService.js.
findings.recommendation: Use a module-level promise: `const stripePromise = createStripeClient(); async function getStripe() { return stripePromise; }`

findings.description: Weak environment variable fallbacks: `process.env.BTC_ADDRESS || 'CONFIGURE_BTC_ADDRESS_IN_ENV'` and similar for Lightning address. In misconfigured deployments, payments would be sent to placeholder string.
findings.recommendation: Fail fast in production: `if (!process.env.BTC_ADDRESS) throw new Error('BTC_ADDRESS env var required')`. Validate all payment-critical env vars at startup.

findings.description: 41 route files in routes/ but only ~8-10 have associated tests. Critical payment routes checkout.js, cryptoCheckout.js, storefront.js lack comprehensive test coverage. ~50 service files, only ~5 have tests.
findings.recommendation: Add Jest tests for all payment-critical routes. Aim for >80% coverage on checkout.js, cryptoCheckout.js, storefront.js, and auth.js. Add integration tests for Stripe webhook handlers.

findings.description: Mixed callback and Promise patterns throughout codebase. database.js exports callback-based API; routes manually wrap in Promises; services use async/await directly. Inconsistency makes control flow hard to follow and error-prone.
findings.recommendation: Promisify the database layer once in databaseHelper.js and use async/await consistently everywhere.

findings.description: Archive directory contains 581-line checkoutProduction.js and other stale files. Commented-out NFT route in server.js (line 101): `// const nftRoutes = require('./routes/nft')`.
findings.recommendation: Move _archive/ contents to git history with a tag. Remove commented-out imports in server.js.

findings.description: Inconsistent file naming: `database-service.js` (kebab-case) vs `orderService.js` (camelCase) vs `aiDiscoveryService.js`. DB column snake_case mixed with JS object camelCase without consistent mapping layer.
findings.recommendation: Adopt camelCase for all JS files. Add a DB result mapper to convert snake_case columns to camelCase properties at the database layer.

findings.description: Magic number in zapService.js line 248: `Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) - 300` (5-minute lookback hardcoded). In storefront.js line 38: `BTC_RATE_TTL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000` is named but inline.
findings.recommendation: Extract to named constants: `const ZAP_POLL_LOOKBACK_SECONDS = 300; const BTC_RATE_CACHE_TTL_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;`

context.summary: The openbazaar-ai backend (Node.js/Express, JavaScript, SQLite) has 18 code quality issues across 4 severity levels. The most urgent are: (1) silent error swallowing in empty catch blocks across payment-critical services, (2) hardcoded BTC price estimate ($60k) in checkout.js that will cause incorrect pricing, (3) copy-pasted database helper functions creating a maintenance hazard, and (4) unimplemented L402/NFT stubs that throw at runtime. High-priority improvements include replacing 1000+ unstructured console.log calls with a structured logger, breaking up 1000+ line files, and standardizing error response formats. Test coverage is critically low — only ~20% of route and service files have tests, with no coverage on the most important payment routes. Quick wins: add error logging to empty catch blocks (30 min), extract magic numbers to constants (1 hr), create centralized databaseHelper.js (1 hr).
